arts.ttl: Sandro Tonali opens up on gambling addiction and Newcastle United support
arts.sum: Sandro Tonali has revealed he visited a factory on Tyneside that helped him change his perspective on gambling following his ban from football. "In the first two months I was detached from everyone, then returning to life, training every day without having the match, I realised that I was paying for what I had done," he told La Repubblica .

arts.ttl: Isak fear, Pope axed, Barnes chance - Newcastle United starting XI decision
arts.sum: Starting XI : Dubravka, Trippier, Krafth, Burn, Hall; Tonali, Miley, Longstaff; Osula, Wilson, Barnes. Starting XI : Pope, Livramento, Krafth, Burn, Hall; Miley, Tonali, Willock; Osula, Wilson, Barnes

arts.ttl: Tottenham to be involved in ‘tug of war’ with Newcastle and Aston Villa for PL midfielder
arts.sum: Champions League chasing clubs Tottenham, Newcastle United and Aston Villa are all looking to strengthen their midfield in the summer transfer window. They have struggled in the Premier League this season but they are ready to invest in the summer to challenge for a place in the Champions League next season. According to GiveMeSport, Tottenham […] The post Tottenham to be involved in ‘tug of war’ with Newcastle and Aston Villa for PL midfielder appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Alexander Isak decision due as Newcastle prepare to take on Brighton in FA Cup
arts.sum: The Newcastle boss admitted that the best-case scenario for Sunday's clash would be having both Isak and Callum Wilson to fit and ready to face the Seagulls, with Howe quashing any doubts surrounding the Magpies number nine. Speaking at his press conference on Friday, Magpies' head coach Eddie Howe admitted he wouldn't be willing to risk the Swedish forward at this stage of the campaign and said United's staff were keeping an eye on the situation.

arts.ttl: Local Hero - The history behind Newcastle United's iconic walkout anthem and goosebump moments - Chronicle Live
arts.sum: Bruce Springstein, Sam Fender, Sting, Eric Clapton, Roger Daltrey, Brian May, Bruce Springsteen, Ringo Starr, Pete Townshend and Ronnie Wood all contributed to the latest re-release, which raised money for Teenage Cancer Trust and Teen Cancer America with the world exclusive heard at St. James' Park for their clash with Wolves. The iconic tune is synonymous with the club and matchdays at St. James' Park, welcoming the teams onto the pitch since the late 1980s and early 1990s.

arts.ttl: Isak might cost Liverpool £120m - Sunday's gossip
arts.sum: Alexander Isak might cost Liverpool more than £120m, Lamine Camara is attracting Premier League interest, Arsenal and Chelsea are linked with Benjamin Sesko, plus more.

The Magpies will be travelling to Wembley Stadium in several weeks to face Liverpool in the Carabao Cup final and are aiming to secure a second visit to the country's grandest stage this season. Newcastle United are aiming to reach the quarter-finals of the FA Cup on Sunday afternoon when they host Brighton & Hove Albion.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United coaching masterclass pays off with bargain buy - and Eddie Howe knows it - Chronicle Live
arts.sum: The 20-year-old was used by Chelsea in midfield, as a left winger and sometimes in the centre of the engine room, but Newcastle boss Howe and his coaching staff tirelessly worked with him last season on the training pitch to perfect his defensive game. Newcastle United boss Eddie Howe has hailed the transformation of Lewis Hall from rookie signing to the Magpies' first choice left-back.

arts.ttl: Sandro Tonali AC Milan transfer truth clear as Newcastle shown £18.1m reality
arts.sum: Reports from Italy in recent months have speculated that a return to his homeland could be on the cards for the Magpies No.8, with Milan, Juventus and Manchester United rumoured to be interested in the player. Tonali recently had an interview in Italy where he spoke about the rumours concerning his future at Newcastle making it clear he does not want to leave St. James' Park.

arts.ttl: Striker Arsenal sold for less than £10m last summer now has more goals than Alexander Isak this season
arts.sum: Arsenal have seen their Premier League title hopes fizzle out amid an injury crisis up front this season. The Gunners are without Bukayo Saka, Gabriel...
